channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In the Ebola epicenter, a gold-mining town reacts with fear, disbelief and grit
item.description: NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reports from Mongbwalu in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The fight to contain the virus faces obstacles from lack of supplies to residents who doubt that the virus is real.

(Image credit: Arsène Mpiana for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: 4 ways to design a dreamy summer, according to a happiness expert
item.description: Don't let the season fly by. Gretchen Rubin, host of the Happier podcast, shares exercises to help you get what you want out of summer. Fill out the printable worksheet and stick it on your fridge.
item.content:encoded: Don't let the season fly by. Gretchen Rubin, host of the Happier podcast, shares exercises to help you get what you want out of summer. Fill out the printable worksheet and stick it on your fridge.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Why are crisis pregnancy centers saying they can 'rule out' ectopic pregnancy?
item.description: Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.
item.content:encoded:
Under President Trump, more federal attention and support has gone towards anti-abortion Christian centers. A watchdog group says many of them mislead patients with promises to "rule out" ectopic pregnancies.

(Image credit: Taylor Glascock for The Washington Post)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: 5 years after the Surfside condo collapse, the toll of the tragedy remains
item.description: Surfside, Florida, is marking five years since a beachfront condominium collapsed, killing 98 people. It was one of the largest structural failures in U.S. history.
item.content:encoded:
Surfside, Florida, is marking five years since a beachfront condominium collapsed, killing 98 people. It was one of the largest structural failures in U.S. history.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Peñaloza

item.title: Appeals court allows Trump administration expanded use of speedy deportations
item.description: A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.
item.content:encoded:
A federal appeals court on Tuesday allowed the Trump administration to resume carrying out speedy deportations of undocumented migrants throughout the United States, not just near the border.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Archaeologists find huge Viking textile production site in Denmark
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.
item.content:encoded:
Archaeologists have discovered a huge Viking Age textile production site in Denmark that dates back more than 1,000 years and underlines the sophistication of Viking society.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
item.description: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.
item.content:encoded:
All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Tension builds between Trump and Senate Republicans, putting GOP agenda on the line
item.description: Divides over what GOP priorities should be ahead of this fall's midterm elections are testing the relationship between President Trump and Senate Republicans.
item.content:encoded:
Divides over what GOP priorities should be ahead of this fall's midterm elections are testing the relationship between President Trump and Senate Republicans.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
item.description: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.
item.content:encoded:
The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel
